evidence: Container lastState shows OOMKilled before the current CrashLoopBackOff state

causes.message: The container's most recent termination was caused by OOMKilled
The workload keeps exceeding its memory limit and cannot stay up long enough to stabilize
Kubelet enters restart backoff after the OOMKilled termination

pod:payments-worker-6c4fd7b6d8-z8qf2: Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff after an OOMKilled container termination

likely_causes: Container memory limit is too low for the workload's startup or steady-state usage
The application has a memory spike or leak that repeatedly triggers kernel OOM enforcement
A recent code or traffic change increased memory consumption beyond the configured limit
suggested_checks: Review container memory requests and limits
Inspect memory usage around startup and peak load
Check whether a recent deployment changed workload memory behavior
Raise the memory limit temporarily to confirm the crashloop is OOM-driven
